title: Peanut Butter Chocolate Bars
summary: Packed with loads of peanuts and sticky dates these bars are the perfect prep-ahead snack to power you through the week. No baking, no fuss, just blend, chill and slice!

method.description: Chuck the peanuts into a food processor and blitz until they look like coarse flour.
Add the dates, peanut butter and a pinch of salt, then blend again until a sticky dough forms.
Break up the dark chocolate and tip into the mixture. Pulse a few more times to mix them through.
Press the mixture into a lined tray or mould, roughly 17cm x 24cm – it should be about 2 cm thick. Chill for 20–30 mins to firm up.
To store, wrap the bars in baking paper and chill or freeze until needed (these last for a week in the fridge and 3 months in the freezer).

tips: Use almond butter instead of peanut if you prefer.
Keep them chilled in a sealed container for up to a week.

chefs.name: Rachel Pamart
chefs.summary: Rachel is the founder of Rawlala, a cold-pressed juicery based in East London. She came to clean, holistic eating by tuning into what made her feel most alive and now she channels that into expert, ingredient-led recipes designed to nourish, energise, and taste damn good.
